title: Music Re-cued
description: Music is random, as it should be.
body:
The last 5 Minecraft updates have added new music, getting increasingly biome-dependent. This clashes with one of the special things of Minecraft music; that music plays randomly, with few exceptions. Many packs have 'fixed' this by removing the new music, but I think that is no good since the new music is great!

My solution here is to reorganize the music into larger, mutually-exclusive pools. This keeps nostalgia in most areas without removing any songs and being more random than 1.21. Is it purely random like in beta? No. I still think it's a better compromise, though.

Here is a summary of the changes:
- **Normal Overworld music (music.game) only plays songs by C418.** If they were in the overworld before Caves & Cliffs, they're here and nowhere else. That means calm, hal, nuance, and piano.
    - This includes other biomes separated out, such as the forest or the taiga.
- **Mountain biomes (all 7, base and peak) are most of the new songs** from 1.18 and many later ones. The mountains got a big overhaul which gives them a new lofty feeling, matching all of this new music.
 
<details>
<summary>Alternate Game  (Mountain) Tracklist</summary>

- An Ordinary Day (Tanioka)
- Comforting Memories (Tanioka)
- Floating Dream (Tanioka)
- Stand Tall (Raine)
- Left to Bloom (Raine)
- One More Day (Raine)
- Ancestry (Raine)
- Aeirie (Raine)
- A Familiar Room (Cherof)
- Eld Unknown (Raine)
- Endless (Raine)
- pokopoko (Tanioka)
- Watcher (Cherof)
- yakusoku (Tanioka)
- Broken Closks (Roddy)
- Fireflies (Roddy)
- Lilypad (Roddy)
- O's Piano (Roddy)

</details>

- **Underwater music is still C418's Update Aquatic soundtrack** from 1.13 with a few additions I found fitting to flesh it out.
 
<details>
<summary>Underwater Tracklist</summary>

- Dragon Fish (C418)
- Shuniji (C418)
- Axolotl (C418)
- Firebugs (Raine)
- Echo in the Wind (Cherof)
- komorebi (Tanioka)

</details>

- **All nether songs play in all the biomes**, instead of being biome-specific (barring the warped forest). I've also moved Deeper by Lena Raine to the nether.
- I did not touch end, boss, and credits themes.
- **There's now a much higher chance of playing the creative tracklist in creative mode**. The rest of the music comes from the main old and new selections.

<details>
<summary>Creative Tracklist</summary>

- Biome Fest (C418)
- Blind Spots (C418)
- Haunt Muskie (C418)
- Aria Math (C418)
- Dreiton (C418)
- Taswell (C418)
- Labyrinthine (Raine)
- Puzzlebox (Cherof)

</details>

- **The menu no longer uses songs for their novelty, instead having a permanent selection**. As with the rest of the pack, separation means they don't play in the mountains.

<details>
<summary>Menu Tracklist</summary>

- Mutation (C418)
- Moog City 2 (C418)
- Beginning 2 (C418)
- Floating Trees (C418)
- Wending (Raine)
- Infinite Amethyst (Raine) (Woah, the New Theme of Minecraft)
- Bromeilad (Cherof)
- Crescent Dunes (Cherof)
- Featherfall (Cherof)
- Below and Above (Roddy)

</details>

- The warped forest is quiet.

<details>
<summary>Warped Forest (rambly)</summary>

Warped forest is an exception to separation. If you listen closely, you can hear very faintly the menu music playing out of tune. The idea is that it's a faint echo or some memory of hearing those songs. It should be quiet and unintrusive so you barely hear it.


</details>

Always feel free to report issues or give feedback in the comments of the PMC upload! (It is linked with the issues.)

That's all. Enjoy! I hope you give this pack a try. =] I think the new composers' music is good, although it's sometimes more themy than people want. That's why they gets the beautiful mountains as the location of their banishment!

*Do not reupload in any capacity.*

**This pack DOESN'T change the actual music! Don't be confused!** ^-^
